triageFlags.title: Applicant Age Below 21
triageFlags.description: The applicant's age is below the minimum threshold for standard underwriting policies.
triageFlags.recommendation: Consider additional scrutiny or require a guarantor to mitigate risks associated with younger applicants.
triageFlags.reasoning: Underwriting policies typically require applicants to be at least 21 years old to ensure financial maturity and stability. Exceptions may require additional safeguards.

triageFlags.title: Debt-to-Income Ratio Exceeds Threshold
triageFlags.description: The applicant's DTI ratio exceeds the maximum allowable threshold for their credit tier.
triageFlags.recommendation: Request additional income verification or consider reducing the loan amount to bring the DTI within acceptable limits.
triageFlags.reasoning: The DTI ratio is a critical measure of affordability. Exceeding the threshold for the applicant's credit tier increases the risk of default.

content.reasoning: The applicant's age and DTI ratio are notable concerns, but the credit score and other financial metrics are within acceptable ranges. Additional verification of affordability is recommended to mitigate risks.
